rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type annexes for port-level assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation annexes for subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model to avoid scope errors.
generic_rules: Assumptions and guarantees must reference valid component ports and features in their respective scopes.
Do not introduce package-qualified names or types not present in the input AADL model.
Ensure AGREE expressions use compatible data types with AADL port declarations.
Avoid generating bounds or constraints not explicitly stated in the requirement.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Constants and equations in AGREE should use valid AADL data types and port names.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes.
Do not assume numeric bounds for ports unless explicitly required by the requirement.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
